name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **INR ₹95 TRIGGER BREACHED-AND-REVERSED WITHIN 24 HOURS** — Tue May 5 set fresh intraday record ₹95.36 (the previously identified Day 2 sustained activation); May 6 PULLED BACK to ₹95.13 (also referenced as ₹94.9770 in some reports), -0.14% session, putting INR back below ₹95 trigger for first time since Apr 30. Yesterday's brief framing of 'Day 2 sustained activation' is REVERSED to 'breached-and-reversed within 24 hours.' Drivers of pullback: (1) Brent collapse to $107.96 reduces oil-import-CAD vector; (2) RBI capital-flow defensive escalation FORMALIZED — capped dealer banks' Net Open Position in INR at $100mn/day, barred domestic banks from offering INR NDF to NRIs and related parties (CONFIRMED RBI directive); (3) DXY -0.4% (Trump-comfortable-with-weak-dollar comments + BlackRock Rieder dovish-Fed-chair speculation) → broader EM-FX strength (USD/KRW -1.4%, USD/BRL -1.5%). FX reserves >$700B (~11mo import cover). The ₹95.50 direct-intervention threshold remains UNTESTED — RBI defended via capital-flow controls only. FII context: CY26 outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore (~$23B+) already exceeds CY25 full-year; FPI ownership of Indian equities ~16%, lowest in nearly 2 decades; DII (SIP-driven) absorbed ~90% of FII selling (~₹1.7 lakh crore YTD). Important nuance: NOP/NDF curbs are defensive containment, not sustainable equilibrium — if Brent breaks $115 again or FII outflows accelerate, ₹95.50 still in play. India 10Y GOI ~6.90% (-10 bps area, steady).
